item.title: India FX Buffer Is Still Robust to Defend Rupee, Economists Say
item.description: India’s foreign-exchange reserves remain strong enough to defend its currency against the Iran-war driven oil shock, with buffers well above stress levels seen during the taper tantrum, according to economists.

item.title: Intertek Inclined to Recommend EQT’s Final £9.2 Billion Offer
item.description: Intertek Group Plc is leaning toward recommending a fourth and final takeover offer from private equity firm EQT AB.

item.title: Japanese Funds Dump Most US Debt Since 2022 as Fed Wagers Flip
item.description: Japanese investors sold the most US sovereign bonds in almost four years as a jump in oil prices led to an abrupt turnaround in Federal Reserve policy bets.

item.title: SoftBank Logs Surprise Profit Jump in Boon for Son’s AI Bets
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. reported a surprise rise in quarterly profit helped by valuation gains on its OpenAI investment.

item.title: Modi’s Belt-Tightening Push Raises Pessimism Around India Stocks
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s push to curb fuel and gold consumption has added to uncertainty in India’s already weak stock markets, raising concerns that moves meant to protect the economy may end up cooling overall spending.

item.title: Iran-Linked LPG Tanker Sails Past US Navy’s Blockade Line
item.description: A liquefied petroleum gas carrier, which has previously ferried Iranian cargoes, has sailed past the blockade boundary announced last month by the US Navy.

item.title: India’s RBI Governor Sees Fuel Price Hike If Oil Stays High
item.description: India may need to increase retail fuel prices if the conflict in the Middle East drags on, Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra said.

item.title: Hitachi Construction Shares Fall After $570 Million Block Trade
item.description: Hitachi Construction Machinery Co. shares extended their drop on Wednesday after major shareholder Hitachi Ltd. offloaded ¥90.1 billion ($571 million) of stock in a block trade.

item.title: FirstRand Picks BofA, RMB to Advise on Sale of UK Aldermore Unit
item.description: FirstRand Ltd. hired Bank of America Corp. and its own investment-banking arm, Rand Merchant Bank, as advisers to help it sell its UK-based Aldermore Group and exit the European market, a spokesperson for the South African lender said.

item.title: UK Bonds Recover Ground After Bruising Selloff
Korea President Clarifies Policy Chief’s ‘Citizen Dividend’ Post
item.description: Korean President Lee Jae Myung said his adviser’s viral post about redistributing profits from the AI boom was meant to start a broader public debate about how a potential tax windfall should be used.

item.title: French Jobless Rate Jumps Above 8% for First Time Since 2021
item.description: French unemployment unexpectedly rose to the highest level in five years, adding to signs the euro area’s second largest economy was already on a weak footing when the Iran war began.

item.title: ABN Amro Profit Beats on Fee Boost, Higher Interest Income
item.description: ABN Amro Bank NV reported quarterly profit that beat analysts’ estimates as fee income surged and lending income also rose.

item.title: Allianz Earnings Rise to Record as Pimco Attracts €38 Billion
item.description: Allianz SE, the owner of bond manager Pacific Investment Management Co., reported a record profit in the first quarter, helped by its property-casualty insurance and the asset management business.

item.title: Investors Rush to Decode New Australian Capital Gains Tax Rules
item.description: Investors are racing to assess the impact of a revamp of Australia’s tax rules on gains from stocks, bonds and property, with wealth managers warning it could upend trust structures and boost demand for safer, income-generating assets.

item.title: Deutsche Telekom Nudges Up 2026 Guidance After T-Mobile Growth
item.description: Deutsche Telekom AG, Europe’s biggest phone carrier, slightly raised its guidance after recording growth in Germany and its US arm, T-Mobile US Inc.

item.title: Siemens to Buy Back €6 Billion Shares Against Tough Backdrop
item.description: Siemens AG will repurchase as much as €6 billion ($7 billion) of shares after orders across the company’s key divisions climbed against a demanding environment.

item.title: Europe’s Oil, Gas Lobbies Urge Flexibility on Storage Targets
item.description: European Union energy lobby groups called for more flexibility in reaching the bloc’s natural gas storage targets, to avoid market pressure during the summer refilling season.

item.title: China’s Profit Outlook to Improve Further, Morgan Stanley Says
item.description: Chinese companies’ second‑quarter profit outlook is set to improve after encouraging first‑quarter results, with rising exports and early signs of reflation boosting revenues, according to Morgan Stanley.

item.title: Boyu Capital Plans to Raise $3 Billion for New China Fund
item.description: Boyu Capital Investment Management Co., fresh off its deal for a majority stake in Starbucks’ China business, plans to raise $3 billion for a new fund, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Gulf Shipping Standoff Hits World’s Most Vulnerable Farmers
item.description: Malawi is an extreme example of the impact on food security from the upheaval in the fertilizer market from the Iran war.

item.title: Japan’s Coal Power Generation Climbs as War Makes LNG Expensive
item.description: Japan’s coal-power generation is rising while natural gas-fired output falls, as conflict in the Middle East chokes supplies of the less-polluting fossil fuel and sends prices higher.

item.title: Sacred Thai Bulls See Bumper Harvest, Prosperity in Ancient Rite
item.description: Thailand’s sacred white oxen forecast ample rains, a bountiful harvest, resurgent foreign trade and a flourishing economy in a centuries-old rite presided over by King Maha Vajiralongkorn and Queen Suthida.

item.title: Thai Rate Panel Warns Against Broad Economic Stimulus as War Unfolds
item.description: Thailand’s central bank cautioned against broad consumption stimulus, saying such a move could constrain fiscal flexibility as the economic fallout from the Middle East conflict continues to unfold.

item.title: Australia Capital Gains Tax Reform Risks Startups, PC Chief Says
item.description: Australia’s plan to scrap a capital gains tax discount risks “unintended consequences” for the startup sector, even as the budget delivered a credible package of productivity reforms, said Danielle Wood, chair of the Productivity Commission.

item.title: Warsh Could Find Inflation Too Hot to Handle
item.description: Pressure for rate cuts is running into a wall of a reality from the Iran war.

item.title: Iran War Will Make EU More Reliant on US Gas Than Ever: IEEFA
item.description: Europe’s reliance on natural gas from the US is expected to surge to a record this year as the country helps offset supplies lost from the Middle East, according to an energy think tank.

item.title: Morgan Stanley Sees Yuan Rally Slowing, Diverging From Bulls
item.description: Morgan Stanley sees room for the yuan to climb further, but disagrees with more bullish views that the currency is significantly undervalued.

item.title: China Sees Its Soy Imports Sliding Just as US Farmers Eye Deals
item.description: China, the world’s top soybean importer, expects purchases to slump in the coming season as its pig herd declines, heightening competition for market share just as the US seeks to ramp up sales.

item.title: Chinese Oil Supertanker Seen Attempting Strait of Hormuz Exit
item.description: A Chinese oil supertanker appears to be sailing out of the Persian Gulf, a rare attempted crossing through the Strait of Hormuz just ahead of talks between US President Donald Trump and Xi Jinping.
